title: fagraea euneura
intro: Fagraea euneura, also known as the white frangipani, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Fagraea euneura include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections. Fagraea euneura is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and capsules.
